page_purpose: Provides users with a consolidated view of all their submitted applications, showing key details and current status to keep them informed.
business_workflow: Application Tracking and Status Monitoring
data_displayed: A table listing each application with columns for ID, USER_NAME, APPLICATION_ID, SENT_ON, and STATUS.

page_purpose: This page allows a banking application user to provide feedback or comments on a specific document. The user can share their thoughts and submit them for review by the relevant stakeholders.
business_workflow: Document Feedback Collection
key_actions: Enter feedback in the 'Share Your Thoughts' field
Navigate back to the document overview
data_displayed: Document title, brief description, and the current user's name

page_purpose: The page provides users with guidance and instructions on how to use the banking application’s document and branch features. It serves as a static help resource, offering explanations, tips, and troubleshooting steps to assist users in navigating the system.
business_workflow: User Guidance and Support for Document and Branch Management
key_actions: Read and understand help content related to documents and branches
Navigate between different help sections or topics
Access contact information or support links for further assistance
data_displayed: Static help text, FAQs, and contact information for support

page_purpose: This page allows a bank customer to search for other users within the bank’s network, view their basic profile information, and initiate interactions such as sending money or adding them as a contact.
business_workflow: Friend Search and Interaction within the banking application.
key_actions: Search for a friend using the search field
Initiate a money transfer to a friend
data_displayed: A list of users matching the search query, showing each friend’s name, account number, and branch information.

page_purpose: The welcome page serves as the first point of entry for users of the banking application, presenting a friendly greeting and a brief overview of the branch they are accessing. It provides quick navigation options to the main features of the system without requiring any input or data entry.
business_workflow: Initial landing and orientation for users, enabling them to access core banking functions and understand the branch context before proceeding to more detailed operations.
key_actions: View branch name and basic information
Navigate to account services or other modules
data_displayed: Welcome message, branch name, and a short description of available services.

page_purpose: This page allows authorized users to enter and submit new branch information into the banking system. It collects essential details such as the branch identifier, name, segment code, and activation status to create a new branch record.
business_workflow: Branch Creation and Management
key_actions: Submit the form to create a new branch
data_displayed: Input fields for Branch ID, Branch Name, Segment Code, and Active status

page_purpose: This page provides administrators with a simple interface to trigger the addition of new data entries for a branch. It displays a confirmation or status message after the operation, allowing the admin to verify the result without interacting with tables or forms.
business_workflow: Branch Data Management
key_actions: Initiate the addition of branch data
View the status or result of the operation
Navigate back to the branch overview or dashboard
data_displayed: Operation status message and brief branch information

page_purpose: This page allows administrators to create a new document template by specifying its activity code, function name, and subject. The template can then be used for generating standardized documents within the banking application.
business_workflow: Document Template Creation
key_actions: Enter activity code for the template
Enter function name associated with the template
Enter subject line for the template
Submit the form to save the new template
Cancel and return to template list
data_displayed: Form fields for activity code, function name, and subject to create a new document template.

page_purpose: This page allows an administrator to create a new employee user account within the banking application by entering personal and contact details.
business_workflow: User Management – onboarding new employees into the system.
key_actions: Submit the form to create the user
Cancel and return to the user list
data_displayed: Form fields for HRMS number, employee name, business unit, mobile number, email ID, and other related user information.

page_purpose: This page enables administrators to monitor and investigate the logs of amendment API queue entries, allowing them to filter records by CRF Number or Amend API Queue ID and view detailed information for troubleshooting or audit purposes.
business_workflow: Amendment Queue Monitoring and Troubleshooting
key_actions: Filter logs by CRF Number or Amend API Queue ID
data_displayed: A table listing amendment API queue log entries, including fields such as Log ID, CRF Number, Amendment ID, status, timestamp, and any error or success messages

page_purpose: This page provides administrators with a view of API queue logs related to document processing. It allows them to filter and review log entries to monitor the status and troubleshoot issues with API calls.
business_workflow: Monitoring and troubleshooting of API queue processing for document-related operations.
key_actions: Filter log entries by AOF Number
Filter log entries by API Queue Id
View detailed information for a selected log entry
data_displayed: A table of API queue log entries showing AOF Number, API Queue Id, status, timestamp, and associated document details.

page_purpose: This page allows administrators to view and filter logs of API service requests related to user and document operations. It provides visibility into the interactions between the application and external services, helping to monitor usage and troubleshoot issues.
business_workflow: API Request Monitoring and Auditing
key_actions: Filter logs by Form Id or AOF Number
View detailed log entry for a specific request
Export the filtered log list to CSV or Excel
Refresh the log list to see the latest entries
data_displayed: A table of API request logs showing request ID, user, document, form ID, AOF number, timestamp, status, and response details

page_purpose: This page allows an administrator to view and modify a single application setting. The admin can update the setting value, add explanatory comments, and toggle a secure flag to control access or behavior of the application.
business_workflow: Application Configuration Management
key_actions: Add or update comments for the setting
Toggle the secure flag on or off
Save changes to persist the new configuration
data_displayed: Current setting value, existing comments, and the status of the secure flag

page_purpose: Provides administrators with a consolidated view of all users, displaying essential employee identifiers and contact information for oversight and quick reference.
business_workflow: User Management – enabling admins to monitor and review user records within the banking system.
key_actions: Export the user list to CSV
data_displayed: A table listing users with columns: ID, HRMSNO, EMPSOL, EMP NAME, and EMPMOBILENO.

page_purpose: This page allows authorized users to edit the metadata of a document or activity template, updating its activity code, function name, and subject line. It provides a simple form for making changes to the template’s core attributes before they are used in document generation or workflow processes.
business_workflow: Template Management – editing and maintaining the configuration of reusable document or activity templates used across the banking application.
key_actions: Modify the activity code of the template
Change the function name associated with the template
Edit the subject line used in generated documents
Cancel and return to the template list
data_displayed: Current values of activity_code, function_name, and subject for the selected template, displayed in editable input fields.

page_purpose: The page provides administrators with a consolidated view of all email and SMS notifications that have been sent in relation to document activities. Users can filter the list by AOF number to quickly locate messages tied to a specific document.
business_workflow: Document Communication Tracking – monitoring and managing notifications sent to stakeholders during document processing.
key_actions: Filter the message list by AOF number
View details of individual email/SMS messages
Export the message list for reporting
data_displayed: A table listing message ID, associated AOF number, activity code, message type (email or SMS), and the recipient email address.

page_purpose: This page presents a comprehensive list of system exceptions that have been logged, enabling administrators to quickly identify and investigate errors that occurred during application operations.
business_workflow: Exception Monitoring and Resolution
key_actions: View exception details by clicking a row
Filter or search the exception list
Export the exception list to CSV
Refresh the list to see new exceptions
data_displayed: A table of exception records showing ID, Module, Function Name, User ID, and AOF Number.

page_purpose: This page allows administrators to view and manage Level 1 edit logs for documents. Users can filter logs by Form ID or AOF Number, review the details of each edit, and perform maintenance actions such as editing or deleting log entries.
business_workflow: Document Amendment Tracking and Audit Logging
key_actions: Filter logs by Form ID or AOF Number
View detailed information for a selected log entry
Edit or update a log entry
data_displayed: A table of Level 1 edit logs showing Log ID, Document ID, Form ID, AOF Number, Edit Timestamp, User who performed the edit, and current status

page_purpose: This page allows an administrator to request the creation or refresh of a master table that aggregates documents and account information for a specific branch. By entering the Branch ID and submitting the form, the system will compile and present the consolidated data for that branch.
business_workflow: Branch Master Table Generation
data_displayed: Summary of documents and account records aggregated for the selected branch, including counts and key details.

page_purpose: This page provides administrators with a quick overview of pending notifications related to documents and accounts, showing how many items are awaiting action on each date. It allows the admin to monitor backlog and plan follow‑up tasks.
business_workflow: Monitoring and managing pending notifications for documents and account activities within the banking system.
key_actions: View detailed list of notifications for a selected date
Mark selected notifications as processed or acknowledged
Export the notification summary to CSV or Excel
Refresh the table to see updated counts
data_displayed: A table listing each date with the corresponding count of pending notifications for documents and accounts.

page_purpose: This page allows an administrator to reset a one‑time task within the banking system, providing a comment to explain the reset. The form is simple, with a single input for the reset comment and a submit button.
business_workflow: One‑Time Task Reset – an administrative process to cancel or restart a task that was previously assigned or completed.
key_actions: Cancel or return to the previous page
data_displayed: Details of the one‑time task being reset (e.g., task ID, description, status) and the reset comment field.

page_purpose: This page provides administrators with a view of debugging information related to document uploads and amendments. It allows them to review upload status, error logs, and other diagnostic details to troubleshoot issues and ensure data integrity.
business_workflow: Document Upload & Amendment Processing – supports the troubleshooting and verification steps that occur after a document or amendment has been submitted.
key_actions: View detailed debug logs for recent document uploads
Download a debug report for offline analysis
Clear or reset the displayed debug information
data_displayed: Debug information such as file names, upload timestamps, status codes, error messages, and any relevant system messages related to Document and Amendment entities.

page_purpose: This page provides administrators with a comprehensive view of system activity logs, allowing them to monitor user actions across various modules. Administrators can filter logs by module to quickly locate relevant events and review details for auditing purposes.
business_workflow: Audit Trail Management and Compliance Monitoring
key_actions: Search logs by user or action
data_displayed: A table of activity log entries showing user, action performed, module affected, timestamp, and IP address

page_purpose: This page allows administrators to view and edit employee user profiles, capturing details such as HRMS number, name, business unit, contact information, and branch association. It serves as the central interface for maintaining accurate employee records within the banking application. The form is designed to be straightforward, with no tables or AJAX, ensuring quick data entry and updates.
business_workflow: User Management – adding or updating employee user profiles within the banking system.
data_displayed: Employee user details such as HRMS number, name, business unit, mobile number, email, branch, role, and other profile fields.

page_purpose: This page displays a historical list of system or policy versions, allowing administrators to review when each version was released and what changes were made.
business_workflow: Supports version tracking and audit for system or policy updates, enabling administrators to monitor release history and reference past configurations.
key_actions: View detailed information for a selected version
Download or export the version details
Print the version list for reporting purposes
data_displayed: A table listing each version with its number, release date, and a brief description of the changes.

page_purpose: The page displays a list of system message templates that are used to notify users about various activities. It allows administrators to review the templates and their associated roles and message types.
business_workflow: Template Management for Notification System
key_actions: Filter templates by role or message type
data_displayed: A table of message templates showing ID, activity code, activity description, role, and message type.

auto_description: Amendment/modification workflow page. collects data via 2 input field(s)

page_purpose: This page displays the breadcrumb trail for the amendment process, helping users understand their current location within the Customer → Document → Amendment hierarchy and providing quick navigation links to each level.
business_workflow: Amendment Tracking and Navigation within the Document Management System.
data_displayed: Breadcrumb trail showing Customer name, Document title, Amendment ID and status

page_purpose: This page shows the details of a document that is part of an amendment and allows the user to attach proof to that amendment. It acts as a confirmation step before the proof is linked to the amendment record.
business_workflow: Document Amendment Proof Attachment
key_actions: View the document and amendment details
data_displayed: Document title, description, amendment reference, current proof status

page_purpose: This page presents a list of customer amendment requests, enabling staff to quickly review pending changes and initiate further processing. It serves as a central hub for accessing amendment details without requiring additional navigation.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Review and Processing
data_displayed: List of amendments for a customer, showing amendment ID, type, status, and date

page_purpose: This page allows staff to locate a customer’s CRF (Credit Request Form) by entering the Customer ID or CRF number, view its current tracking status, and make necessary amendments to the tracking information.
business_workflow: Customer CRF Tracking Amendment
key_actions: Enter Customer ID or CRF number to search
data_displayed: Current CRF tracking details such as status, last updated date, and related document references.

page_purpose: This page displays the details of a customer account amendment, allowing staff to review the requested changes before taking further action.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Review and Approval
data_displayed: Customer name, account number, branch information, amendment type, requested changes, amendment status, effective date

page_purpose: The Amend Dashboard allows bank staff to locate and review amendment requests for customer accounts. Users can filter requests by CRF Number or Customer ID and view key details before proceeding with further actions.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Management – tracking, reviewing, and processing amendment requests for account changes.
key_actions: Search amendment requests by CRF Number or Customer ID
View a list of pending amendment requests
Open detailed view of a selected amendment
Initiate or approve an amendment request
data_displayed: A table of amendment requests showing Created At, Application Id, CRF Number, Customer Id, and Account No.

auto_description: Amendment/modification workflow page. collects data via 1 input field(s)

page_purpose: This page allows a user to upload a supporting image for an amendment request, such as a scanned document or photo, and submit it for further processing.
business_workflow: Supports the amendment processing workflow by attaching necessary documentation to a customer’s amendment record before it moves to the approval stage.
data_displayed: Amendment details (ID, customer name, amendment type) and instructions for uploading the supporting image

page_purpose: This page allows a banking staff member to modify the type of an existing amendment for a customer. The user can review the amendment details and update the type before saving the changes.
business_workflow: Amendment Modification Process
data_displayed: Customer name, amendment ID, current amendment type, amendment status

page_purpose: This page allows a user to submit an amendment to a customer’s account or document by uploading the amended file and providing the necessary credentials. The submission initiates the internal approval workflow for the amendment.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Submission and Approval
data_displayed: Current amendment details and any existing document preview

page_purpose: This page provides a read‑only view of the tracking details for a specific amendment request, allowing users to see its current status, history, and related user information.
business_workflow: Amendment Tracking and Status Monitoring
data_displayed: Amendment ID, type, current status, submission and last updated dates, assigned user details, and any tracking comments or notes

page_purpose: Allows a user to upload supporting documents for an amendment request related to a customer or account. The page captures amendment details and provides a preview of the uploaded file before submission.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Documentation – the process of submitting and reviewing supporting documents for an amendment to a customer’s account.
key_actions: Select the type of amendment and enter related data
Upload a document (e.g., ID, address proof) and preview it via AJAX
Submit the amendment request for approval
data_displayed: Amendment type selection, customer/account identifiers, file upload field with preview, and any pre‑filled amendment data.

page_purpose: This page allows a user to upload and preview a supporting document for an amendment request, then submit it for processing.
business_workflow: It supports the document submission step within the amendment approval process for bank accounts.
key_actions: Upload a document file for the amendment
data_displayed: Amendment request details such as account number, amendment type, and current status are shown to the user.

page_purpose: This page provides a simple interface for toggling the visibility of a sensitive document. Users can either mask the document to hide confidential information or unmask it to view the full details, ensuring compliance with privacy policies.
business_workflow: Document Privacy Management – controlling whether a document’s sensitive content is visible to users based on role and regulatory requirements.
data_displayed: Document ID, Document Name, Current Mask Status, Associated Branch

page_purpose: This page provides a consolidated view of all amendment requests awaiting review or action. Users can quickly identify pending or completed amendments and generate printable copies for record‑keeping or further processing.
business_workflow: Amendment Request Review and Documentation
data_displayed: A table of amendment requests showing request ID, customer name, account number, branch, status, and request date.

page_purpose: This page allows authorized users to view a customer document and mask the Aadhaar number before it is shared or stored, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations. It provides a visual interface for selecting the sensitive area and applying a mask directly on the document image.
business_workflow: Document masking for compliance – the process of reviewing customer documents, identifying sensitive Aadhaar information, and applying a mask to protect personal data before further processing or distribution.
key_actions: View the document image on a canvas
Select the Aadhaar number area and apply a mask
Save the masked document to the system
Download the masked document for external use
data_displayed: Document image with overlay, customer name, document type, and current status

auto_description: Displays data in 1 table(s). collects data via 2 input field(s)

page_purpose: This page allows users to attach a supporting image to an existing amendment record. By uploading a note image, the amendment can be updated with visual evidence or documentation that supports the change.
business_workflow: Amendment Processing – attaching supporting documentation to an amendment before it moves to the next approval stage.
key_actions: Upload a note image for the amendment
Cancel or return to the amendment overview
data_displayed: Amendment details such as amendment ID, type, status, and the associated document reference.

page_purpose: This page presents a concise header view of a customer amendment request, allowing users to quickly assess key details before proceeding to further actions such as approval or detailed review.
business_workflow: Amendment Processing – specifically the review and approval stage of customer account amendments.
data_displayed: Amendment header information including amendment ID, type, date, status, and basic customer details such as name and account number.

page_purpose: This page allows a user to review the details of a customer amendment request and decide whether to hold or reject it.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Approval
data_displayed: Amendment details including customer name, amendment type, status, date, description, and any attached documents.

page_purpose: This page presents the details of a pending amendment request to the user, allowing them to review the requested changes and decide whether to approve or reject the amendment.
business_workflow: Amendment Review and Approval Process
data_displayed: Amendment details including customer name, account number, requested changes, branch information, and current status.

page_purpose: This page displays a single action button that allows users to submit or confirm an amendment to a non‑performing credit (NPC) record. It acts as the final step before the amendment is processed in the system.
business_workflow: Non‑Performing Credit Amendment Approval
key_actions: Click the button to submit the NPC amendment
Navigate back to the amendment overview page
data_displayed: None

page_purpose: The dashboard provides a consolidated view of pending customer amendment requests, allowing staff to quickly locate and review changes needed for customer records. Users can filter requests by CRF number or customer ID and initiate further processing steps.
business_workflow: Customer Amendment Processing
key_actions: Filter amendment requests by CRF number or Customer ID
data_displayed: A table of pending amendment requests showing Created At, CRF Number, Customer ID, Account Number, and Source

page_purpose: This page serves as a navigation hub for users to access document and amendment related sections within the banking application. It provides quick links to view existing documents, review amendment requests, and manage related workflows. The layout is intentionally simple, with no tables or input fields, focusing solely on navigation.
business_workflow: Supports the Document Amendment Process by allowing users to move between document listings, amendment requests, and related detail views. It facilitates the review and approval steps that occur after an amendment is submitted.
key_actions: Navigate to the Document overview page
Navigate to the Amendment overview page
Open a specific document or amendment for review
data_displayed: Navigation links and brief labels for Document and Amendment sections; no tabular data is displayed on this page.

page_purpose: This modal allows a branch user to upload a document that supports an amendment to a Non-Performance Category (NPC) record. The uploaded file is attached to the NPC change request for review and approval.
business_workflow: Document submission for NPC amendment
key_actions: Select and upload a supporting document file
data_displayed: Branch name and NPC reference information

page_purpose: This page allows a user to enable or disable an amendment for a specific document or account. It provides a simple toggle interface so the user can quickly change the amendment status without navigating away from the current view.
business_workflow: Amendment Status Management – the process of activating or deactivating amendments on documents or accounts within the banking system.
key_actions: Toggle the amendment status using the switch
Submit the change to update the amendment record
View the current amendment status and related document/account details
data_displayed: Current amendment status for the selected document/account, along with basic identifiers for the document and account

page_purpose: The Aadhaar Masking Dashboard provides a visual overview of documents with masked Aadhaar information, allowing users to quickly verify compliance and access masked documents for archival purposes.
business_workflow: Supports the compliance verification and archival process for documents containing Aadhaar data, ensuring sensitive information is masked before storage or sharing.
data_displayed: Visual preview of masked documents, including document title, branch, and masked Aadhaar number, displayed on a canvas.

page_purpose: This page allows bank staff to assign archival identifiers to documents, organize them into storage boxes, and generate printable barcode labels for physical filing.
business_workflow: Document Archival and Storage Management
key_actions: Search and select documents to archive
Assign an archival (AOF) number and box number
Generate and print barcode labels for archived documents
data_displayed: List of documents pending archival, showing AOF Number, Account Number, Customer ID, Customer Name, and current Barcode Number

page_purpose: This page allows users to locate and review archived documents associated with a specific AOF number. By searching for an AOF, users can quickly see where the document is stored and which customer it belongs to, facilitating retrieval or audit processes.
business_workflow: Document Retrieval and Archival Management
key_actions: Search archived records by AOF number
View detailed record information including barcodes and customer details
Request retrieval of a specific archived document
Print or export the list of archival records
data_displayed: A table of archived records showing BOX BARCODE, FILE BARCODE, AOF NUMBER, CUSTOMER NAME, and CUSTOMER ID

page_purpose: This page allows users to view and manage archived document images associated with a specific application. Users can filter the list by AOF Number or ID and access details for each image.
business_workflow: Document Archival and Retrieval
key_actions: Filter images by AOF Number or ID
data_displayed: A table of archived document images showing serial number, AOF Number, applicant sequence, image type, and side (front/back).

page_purpose: This page provides a consolidated view of archived documents within the banking system, allowing users to quickly locate and reference records by barcode and customer information. It serves as a central hub for reviewing and managing archived files without requiring additional input.
business_workflow: Archival Management – tracking, reviewing, and retrieving archived documents for compliance and audit purposes.
key_actions: Search and filter records by barcode or customer
Import new archival entries via Excel upload
data_displayed: A table of archived documents showing BOX BARCODE, FILE BARCODE, AOF NUMBER, CUSTOMER NAME, and CUSTOMER ID.

page_purpose: This page provides users with a consolidated view of their archived applications, allowing them to quickly review key details such as application number, customer information, and account status. It serves as a reference point for past submissions and related documents.
business_workflow: Archival Application Review
data_displayed: List of archived user applications with columns: AOF Number, Customer ID, Account No, Name, TYPE

page_purpose: This page allows a user to view the details of an archived Excel record. It displays metadata such as file name, upload date, and status, and provides options to download the file or navigate back. No editing is possible on this page.
business_workflow: Archival Record Review
data_displayed: Metadata of the archived Excel record (file name, upload date, size, status, uploader, etc.)

page_purpose: This page displays a list of pending Aadhaar mask requests for documents that need to be processed before issuance. It allows users to review the details of each request and take action accordingly.
business_workflow: Document Verification and Masking of Aadhaar Numbers
data_displayed: A list of pending Aadhaar mask requests showing AOF Number, Applicant ID, Applicant Sequence, ID Proof Number, and Address Proof Number.

page_purpose: This page allows a user to authenticate into the banking system using a domain-specific initialization key and password. It provides a secure entry point for branch or domain users to access their banking operations.
business_workflow: User Authentication for Branch/Domain Access
data_displayed: Dynamic golden text message (e.g., welcome or celebratory message) displayed via AJAX

page_purpose: The login page authenticates users to the banking application by collecting an initialization key, domain ID, and password. It verifies credentials and grants access to the system’s secured features.
business_workflow: User Authentication and Access Control
data_displayed: Login form fields and any authentication error messages

page_purpose: This page enables bank staff to create a new customer account by entering key details such as constitution type, form ID, number of account holders, and uploading supporting documents. It links the new account to an existing customer and submits the information for processing.
business_workflow: Account Creation
key_actions: Enter account details (constitution, form ID, number of holders),
Upload required documents via file input,
Select or enter Customer ID to associate the account,
Submit the form to create the account,
data_displayed: Input fields for account details and file upload; no tabular data is displayed.

page_purpose: The Add Account Applicant page lets a bank employee capture all necessary personal and document details for a new account applicant. The form collects information such as PF type, PAN card number, and uploads the PAN card image, preparing the data for the account opening process.
business_workflow: Account Creation – onboarding a new applicant into the bank’s account opening pipeline.
data_displayed: Form fields for applicant personal information, PF type selection, PAN card number inputs, and document upload controls.

page_purpose: This page enables bank staff to assign and manage airway bill numbers for batches of documents, allowing them to track shipments and generate printable reports.
business_workflow: Supports the document shipment and logistics workflow by linking document batches to airway bills and facilitating batch tracking and reporting.
key_actions: View and filter existing document batches
Add or update airway bill numbers for a selected batch
Generate and print batch shipment reports
data_displayed: A table of document batches showing ID, Batch No, Airway bill no, Courier Name, and Created Batch Date, along with input fields for selecting a batch and entering an airway bill number.

page_purpose: This page allows a bank employee to capture detailed financial information for a customer, such as income, expenses, assets, and liabilities, as part of the customer profile or loan application process.
business_workflow: Customer Financial Information Capture for Credit Assessment
key_actions: Enter and edit 22 financial detail fields for a customer
Save the financial information to the system
Submit the information for approval or further processing
Navigate back to the customer overview
data_displayed: Customer name, form ID, duration (years/months), and the 22 financial metrics entered in the form

page_purpose: Enables a bank customer to add or update nominee information for their account, ensuring all required personal and documentation details are captured in compliance with regulatory requirements.

Upload identity and address proof documents
Review entered nominee information before submission

Submit the nominee details for processing
Cancel or reset the upload form

page_purpose: This page allows a banking user to assign a risk classification to a specific transaction or account. The user can enter details such as the source of risk, comments, and whether the transaction is inward or outward, and then submit or save the information for compliance review.
business_workflow: Risk Classification for Transaction/Account – a compliance step that tags transactions with risk categories before further processing or approval.

Save a draft of the classification
Cancel and return to the previous screen

business_workflow: Risk assessment and classification during customer onboarding or periodic review, supporting compliance and due‑diligence processes.

Cancel and return to customer overview

page_purpose: This page displays a breadcrumb navigation trail that helps users understand their current location within the banking application and provides quick links to parent sections. It enhances usability by offering context and easy navigation without requiring additional input.
business_workflow: Facilitates user navigation across banking modules such as Accounts, Customers, and Documents, enabling quick access to related sections and improving overall user experience.

View the current navigation path for context awareness
Use breadcrumb links to quickly jump to higher-level sections

page_purpose: Displays the call center declaration statement that outlines responsibilities and compliance requirements for call center staff.
business_workflow: Supports compliance and onboarding by presenting the declaration to call center agents before they begin handling customer interactions.
data_displayed: The full text of the call center declaration statement.

page_purpose: This page allows call center staff to initiate an initial funding transaction for a customer’s account. Users select the type of funding, enter the account details and amount, and submit the request to create a funding document.
business_workflow: Initial Funding Creation for Customer Accounts
key_actions: Select the type of initial funding (e.g., deposit, transfer)
Enter the customer account number and view the current available balance
Submit the funding request to create a Document and update the Account
data_displayed: Current available balance for the selected account

page_purpose: This page presents a visual grid of document categories linked to customer accounts, enabling users to quickly identify and access relevant documents. It offers a high‑level snapshot of category status and associated account or customer information without requiring any data entry or dynamic updates.
business_workflow: Document Management – categorizing, reviewing, and accessing documents tied to accounts and customers.
key_actions: View detailed information for a document category
Navigate to the associated account or customer profile
Download or preview documents within a selected category
Mark a document or category as reviewed
Navigate to related customer or account information
data_displayed: A grid of document categories, each showing the category name, number of documents, status indicator, and identifiers for the linked account or customer.

page_purpose: The dashboard allows users to view and search their submitted banking applications, providing quick access to key details such as application number, customer name, and account information.
business_workflow: Application Tracking and Management

data_displayed: A table listing the user's applications with columns for AOF Number, Name, TYPE, Account Type, and Scheme Code.

page_purpose: This page allows a branch user to configure the declaration text and related account settings for a customer’s account, such as GPA requirement and two‑way sweep options. It provides a single form for entering or updating these values before they are applied to the account.
business_workflow: Account Configuration – setting declaration and operational flags for a customer account within a branch.

data_displayed: Current declaration text, GPA required value, two‑way sweep status for the selected account

page_purpose: This page enables bank staff to create a personalized welcome or delight document for a customer by entering the customer’s ID and account number. The system then generates the document for review or download.

page_purpose: This page allows bank staff to view and manage dispatch applications for customer documents. Users can filter the list by customer type and airway bill number, review application details, and initiate or track the dispatch process.
business_workflow: Dispatching customer documents and tracking shipment status for banking applications.

View detailed information for a selected dispatch application
Mark an application as dispatched or send it to the customer
Refresh or reload the dispatch list
Export or print the dispatch application list
data_displayed: A list of pending or sent dispatch applications showing AOF Number, Name, Account Type, and Sent On dates.

page_purpose: Provides a read‑only view of a selected business entity—Document, Branch, or Customer—so that users can review its key attributes and status without making changes.
business_workflow: Entity Detail Retrieval and Review

data_displayed: Customer's account information (e.g., account number, type), pre-filled personal details, and a summary of the entered initial funding information.

Submit the reviewed details for approval
data_displayed: Customer name and ID, Account number and type, Initial funding date, Funding amount, Transaction reference, and a preview of the uploaded cheque image

page_purpose: This page allows a user to review and update the nominee details associated with their banking account. It provides a form to confirm personal information, relationship, address, and witness signatures before final submission.
business_workflow: Nominee Management – updating or adding nominee information for a customer account.
key_actions: View and edit nominee personal details (name, relationship, address)
Submit the updated nominee details for processing
data_displayed: Current nominee information including name, relationship to applicant, address, and signature status.

page_purpose: This page allows call‑center staff to review and update a customer’s KYC information. The agent can toggle fields such as place of birth, nationality, and residence country to indicate whether the data has been verified or needs updating before the application proceeds.
business_workflow: Customer KYC Review and Update
key_actions: Toggle KYC fields to mark them as verified or needing update
View current KYC status and application details
data_displayed: Customer personal details, current KYC status, application reference, and toggle controls for each KYC field

page_purpose: This page allows a user to declare that they possess proof of a specific document or account related to a customer. The user can toggle a proof checkbox and submit the declaration for processing.
business_workflow: Document Verification and Proof Declaration
data_displayed: Details of the document, account, and customer associated with the declaration (e.g., document type, account number, customer name).

page_purpose: The page provides a dashboard for staff to review and manage account opening applications, allowing them to filter and inspect customer details, identify PAN/UPI mismatches, and take action on each application.
business_workflow: Account Opening Application Processing – verification of customer documents and data consistency before approval or rejection.
key_actions: Filter applications by AOF number or mobile number
data_displayed: A list of account opening applications showing ID, Customer Name, PAN/UPI mismatch status, AOF Number, and Mobile Number.

page_purpose: This page displays the details of an account opening request and allows the user to review and adjust the requested amount before it is processed.
business_workflow: Account Opening Review and Approval
data_displayed: Account opening request details including applicant information, account type, requested amount, and current status.

page_purpose: This page allows bank staff to review and manage a customer’s identity and supporting documents during the account opening or amendment process. It presents a detailed form for uploading, toggling, and validating various ID proofs and related information.
business_workflow: Customer Document Verification and Approval
key_actions: Upload and review ID proof images
Toggle requirement of ID proof images
Enter and validate ID proof card numbers
Set expiry dates for passports or driving licenses
data_displayed: Customer personal details, account information, document status, uploaded images, and expiry dates

auto_description: Npc (non-personal customer) operations page. collects data via 1 input field(s)

page_purpose: This page enables banking staff to update privilege settings for a specific account or document by entering its AOF number. Once submitted, the system processes the change and confirms the updated privilege status to the user.
business_workflow: Privilege Update Process for Customer Accounts
key_actions: Enter the AOF number to identify the target account or document
Submit the form to trigger the privilege update
View a confirmation message indicating success or failure of the update
Navigate back to the privilege overview or dashboard
data_displayed: A single input field for the AOF number and a submit button; after submission, a success or error message is displayed.

page_purpose: This page allows authorized users to update the account details associated with a privilege request, ensuring that the correct funding source and account numbers are recorded before the request proceeds to approval.
business_workflow: Supports the process of updating account details for a privilege application, ensuring accurate account information before the application is reviewed and approved.
key_actions: Enter or modify the funding source
Submit the updated details for approval
data_displayed: Current account details (Funding, Account No, SA Account No, TD Account No, CA Account No) for the privilege request

page_purpose: This page allows authorized staff to update the signature information for a customer or account, including setting a signature flag that indicates the status of the signature. The user can review the current signature details and submit changes for processing.
business_workflow: Customer Signature Management – updating and validating signature records for accounts.
key_actions: Set or clear the signature flag
Submit the signature update for approval
Reset the form to clear entered data
data_displayed: Current account and customer signature status, including existing signature details and flag state

page_purpose: This page allows a branch user to review a pending amendment request, view its details, and decide whether to hold or reject the request.
business_workflow: Amendment Review and Decision
key_actions: Open hold modal to set hold reason
data_displayed: Details of the amendment request including customer name, account number, requested changes, associated documents, and current status.

page_purpose: This page displays the risk assessment details for a customer or account, allowing the user to view key risk metrics and toggle the visibility of gross income information. It provides a concise overview of the customer's risk profile without using tables, making it easy to review at a glance.
business_workflow: Risk Assessment Review
data_displayed: Risk assessment metrics, customer and account information, transaction summary, and gross income (when toggled).

page_purpose: This page allows a compliance or risk officer to review and confirm the key risk indicators for a customer’s account. The officer can toggle the presence of risk factors such as income, source of funds, net worth, occupation, and any additional occupation details before finalizing the risk assessment.
business_workflow: Customer Risk Assessment Review
key_actions: Toggle risk indicators for gross income, source of funds, net worth, occupation, and other occupation
Submit the reviewed risk details for approval
Request additional information or clarification from the customer
Flag the account for further investigation if risk thresholds are exceeded
data_displayed: Customer risk profile information including gross income, source of funds, net worth, occupation, and other occupation details

auto_description: Npc (non-personal customer) operations page. collects data via 4 input field(s)

page_purpose: This page displays a report of document amendments, allowing users to filter the list by a start date and export the data for further analysis or record-keeping.
business_workflow: Document Amendment Tracking and Reporting
key_actions: Filter the amendment list by selecting a start date
View detailed information for each amendment record
Export the displayed table to a downloadable format
Navigate to related documents or amendment details via AJAX
Refresh or reload the table data
data_displayed: A table listing amendment records with columns such as Document ID, Amendment ID, Amendment Date, Status, and related metadata.

page_purpose: This page provides a read‑only view of the API queue logs, allowing users to monitor the status and details of API requests processed by the system. It serves as a quick reference for troubleshooting and audit purposes.
business_workflow: Monitoring and troubleshooting of API queue processing
data_displayed: A list of API queue log entries showing timestamp, request ID, status, and brief message

page_purpose: The page delivers a daily snapshot of document processing activity for a branch, showing how many documents were completed, how many remain pending, and a short‑term backlog. It lets staff quickly gauge daily performance and spot any outstanding items that need attention.
business_workflow: Daily Document Processing Summary
key_actions: View the number of documents completed today
View the list of documents pending today
View the 2‑day backlog of pending documents
Refresh the displayed data via AJAX
Navigate to detailed views of individual documents
data_displayed: Counts of completed documents, pending documents, and 2‑day backlog, possibly broken down by branch or account type.

page_purpose: The L3 Report page allows users to generate a list of documents for a specific branch starting from a chosen date. It provides a quick snapshot of document activity and status for compliance or audit purposes.
business_workflow: Supports the document audit and compliance reporting process by filtering and presenting branch‑specific documents for review and record‑keeping.
key_actions: Select a start date to filter documents
View the generated document list in a table
Export the report to CSV or Excel
Print the report for physical records
data_displayed: A table listing documents with columns such as Document ID, Document Type, Status, Branch Name, Creation Date, and any relevant metadata.

page_purpose: This page allows authorized personnel to create a new user account by entering employee details such as HRMS number, name, business unit, contact information, and other required attributes.
business_workflow: User Management – onboarding new employees into the banking system.
key_actions: Enter employee details into the form
Submit the form to create the user account
Reset the form to clear all fields
Navigate back to the user list or dashboard
data_displayed: Input fields for HRMS number, employee name, business unit, mobile number, email ID, and other user attributes.

page_purpose: This page allows a user to enter and submit new employee details into the banking system, creating a new user record based on LDAP and internal identifiers.
business_workflow: User Management – adding a new employee/user to the system.
key_actions: Fill in first, middle, and last names
Submit the form to create the user
data_displayed: Form fields for empldapuserid, empsol, emp_first_name, emp_middle_name, and emp_last_name

page_purpose: This page provides a consolidated view of all users registered in the User Access Management (UAM) system, allowing administrators to quickly review user identifiers and associated HR details.
business_workflow: User Management – specifically, the oversight and monitoring of UAM user accounts.
key_actions: View the list of UAM users
Refresh the user list via AJAX
Navigate to individual user profiles for detailed information
data_displayed: A table listing each UAM user’s ID, HRMS number, employee solution ID, employee name, and LDAP user ID.

page_purpose: The UAM Dashboard displays a list of users and allows authorized personnel to view and modify access flags such as NORMAL_FLAG, PRIORITY_FLAG, and NR_FLAG. It serves as a central interface for managing user attributes that influence system permissions and workflow routing.
business_workflow: User Management and Access Control – the page supports the process of reviewing and updating user access flags to ensure correct role assignment and priority handling within the banking application.
key_actions: View the list of users and their current flag settings
Filter or search users by HRMSNO, EMP NAME, or flag values
Edit NORMAL_FLAG, PRIORITY_FLAG, or NR_FLAG for selected users
Save changes to update user access attributes
Refresh the table to reflect updated data
data_displayed: A table listing users with columns for ID, HRMSNO, EMP NAME, EMPLDAPUSERID, and the current NORMAL_FLAG status, along with input fields for adjusting priority and NR flags.

page_purpose: This page allows authorized users to view and update the personal and employment details of an employee within the banking system. It serves as the central form for capturing HRMS number, contact information, and business unit assignments.
business_workflow: User Management – Employee Onboarding and Profile Maintenance
data_displayed: Employee personal and employment information including HRMS number, name, business unit, mobile number, email address, and related document references.

page_purpose: This page displays a list of users that have been marked for deletion, allowing authorized personnel to review and restore them to the active user base. It provides a quick view of key employee identifiers and LDAP details for verification before restoration.
business_workflow: User Management – specifically the restoration of previously deleted user accounts.
key_actions: Export the list of deleted users
data_displayed: A table of deleted users showing ID, HRMS number, employee serial (EMPSOL), employee name, and LDAP user ID.
